description: Toggle mode with silence detection is still not wired into runtime behavior. Static review found the hotkey callback in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs` always behaves like hold-to-talk, while `prefs.mode`, `prefs.silence_seconds`, and `prefs.silence_rms` are not used to auto-stop based on silence. `KeysHandle::set_enabled()` is also never applied from saved preferences, so left/right chord settings do not yet affect runtime behavior.
design: Implement toggle mode on top of the shared session/audio path used by hold-to-talk. The hotkey callback should branch on `prefs.mode`, start capture once on activation, and stop either on the next activation chord or when silence has exceeded the configured threshold. Use `AudioHandle::set_silence_threshold()`, poll silence duration from the active capture, and apply left/right chord preference changes through `KeysHandle::set_enabled()` so saved settings control runtime behavior.

description:
DESCRIPTION
The follow-up review on `stt-uwb` found that the new macOS permission tests in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/permissions.rs` are better than the previous broad enum-shape checks, but they still do not prove the most safety-critical behavior. Tests such as `test_microphone_request_opens_settings()` and `test_check_accessibility_uses_safe_api()` currently assert return values only, so a future refactor could reintroduce brittle shell commands, undeclared local dependencies, or other unsafe probing while the test suite still passes.

This matters because the project plan and `permissions-safety` workstream explicitly require side-effect-free permission handling and safe onboarding for microphone and accessibility access. The integration boundary lives in the macOS-specific permission code in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/permissions.rs`, especially the command-launching path for `request_microphone()` and the safe accessibility-check path that should remain based on `AXIsProcessTrusted()` rather than shell or synthetic-input fallbacks.
design:
DESIGN
Keep this issue focused on testability and contract enforcement for the existing `Permissions` API in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/permissions.rs`. Refactor the macOS-specific helpers behind small, testable boundaries if needed so tests can verify the exact onboarding command and safe-check implementation path without launching real system UI during unit tests.

Preserve the current product behavior from `stt-uwb`: microphone status intentionally degrades to `PermissionState::Undetermined`, microphone request uses settings-based onboarding, and accessibility checks stay side-effect free. If helper extraction is needed, keep the public permission commands exposed through `apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs` stable.

close_reason: Added 9 regression tests for PTT session persistence contract that validate persisted entry source, typed flag, and session metadata by reading directly from the database
description: Static review of stt-t4k uncovered a remaining test gap around the real push-to-talk persistence contract. The backend runtime in apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs and apps/web/src-tauri/src/session.rs now creates sessions, persists typed or untyped entries, and ends sessions with computed totals, but the added tests mostly assert session totals and session mode rather than reading persisted Entry rows. That leaves the key integration boundary under-tested: whether the runtime actually writes the correct entry.source and typed values for hold vs toggle sessions and for typing-success vs typing-fallback paths. This matters because stt-1j5 and later HUD and QA work build on the same runtime path, and a regression here would silently corrupt session history without necessarily breaking existing total-count assertions. The relevant contract lives across apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs, apps/web/src-tauri/src/session.rs, apps/web/src-tauri/src/db.rs, and the Tauri command helpers start_session, end_session, add_typed_entry, and add_untyped_entry.
design: Keep this issue focused on regression coverage for the existing PTT persistence behavior rather than adding new product behavior. The tests should exercise the shared runtime and session boundary that owns session creation, entry persistence, and end-of-session totals. Prefer validating persisted database rows or a small extracted helper over relying only on indirect aggregate assertions. Preserve the current ownership split: lib.rs drives the hotkey runtime, SessionManager in session.rs owns active-session state, and db.rs defines the persisted contract for Session and Entry. Sequence this after stt-t4k; downstream runtime work like stt-1j5 should be able to rely on these tests as a guardrail rather than re-proving the same persistence semantics.
title: Add regression tests for PTT session persistence contract

acceptance_criteria:
1. Record capture writes into the same buffer/state later consumed by `get_and_clear_chunk()` and `stop()`.
2. Backend chunking honors `prefs.record.chunk_seconds` without requiring the frontend to fake chunk timing.
3. Stopping record mode flushes any final buffered audio instead of dropping it.
4. Transcript file and DB entry creation stay in sync for every processed chunk.
5. The reopen rationale is documented in the issue history.
description: Long-form record capture is not complete enough to treat as done. In `apps/web/src-tauri/src/audio.rs`, `RecordCapture::start()` spawns a capture loop using thread-local state instead of the `self` state later read by `get_and_clear_chunk()` and `stop()`. `run_record_capture_loop()` ignores `_chunk_duration_ms`, so backend chunking is not actually enforced, and `stop_record_mode()` discards the final `Vec<f32>` returned by `record.stop()`, risking loss of the trailing audio. Reopened because the original close reason claimed rolling transcript files, session management, and DB persistence were fully implemented.
design: Finish the record-capture backend before relying on UI or QA. Share live capture state between the CPAL callback and `RecordCapture` methods, enforce chunk boundaries based on `prefs.record.chunk_seconds`, flush the final partial chunk on stop, and keep transcript-file and DB persistence on the same path so no capture data is silently lost.
title: Implement long-form record mode audio capture

description: Owns trustworthy model SHA-256 verification and the complete backend-frontend contract for model management on the /settings page. The settings route already renders model UI, but the backend is incomplete: api.ts calls get_model_statuses, get_current_model, and verify_model, but none of these commands exist in the Rust backend. The /settings page loader will crash at runtime when it tries to invoke get_model_statuses. Additionally, the SHA-256 hashes in stt.rs get_model_info() are placeholder hex values (e.g., c78c5ea52d4523...e5e5e5) that will not match real Whisper model files. Three model profiles are supported: EnglishSmall (small.en), MultilingualSmall, and MultilingualMedium.
design: Two-phase approach: stt-bih first replaces placeholder SHA-256 constants with verified hashes from real Whisper model files and ensures get_model_info() returns accurate data. Then stt-j53 implements the three missing Tauri commands (get_model_statuses, get_current_model, verify_model), registers them in lib.rs, and ensures the /settings loader and Verify buttons work end-to-end. The frontend api.ts types (ModelStatus interface) define the contract the backend must match. Keep the model loading and transcription path in stt.rs stable while adding the status/verification surface.

acceptance_criteria:
1. Starting from `/record` invokes backend record-mode capture rather than creating DB rows directly.
2. Stopping from `/record` invokes backend stop and persists the final session state.
3. The page displays live transcript chunks and real record status from the backend.
4. Rotation count and current-session state reflect real runtime state.
5. Manual QA can exercise record mode from the UI without relying on fake session rows.
description: The `/record` route in `apps/web/src/routes/record.tsx` currently creates and updates database sessions directly instead of using the actual backend record-mode commands (`start_record_mode`, `stop_record_mode`, `transcribe_record_chunk`, `get_record_status`). As a result, the UI can claim recording has started while no long-form audio capture, chunk transcription, transcript-file writing, or rotation is happening. Runtime state is also inferred from stale open sessions instead of the live backend capture state.
design: Keep this issue focused on frontend/runtime integration. Replace the direct `api.sessions.create/update` flow with a record-mode API surface that wraps the existing Tauri commands. Use backend status instead of `ended_at == null` heuristics, poll or subscribe for live transcript chunks, and update the page so rotation count and transcript state come from real runtime events rather than placeholder local state.

acceptance_criteria:
1. A new epic named `Workstream: lefthook` exists with child issues covering hook activation, docs, tooling standards, CI parity, and agent review messaging.
2. Dependencies encode the intended execution order so agents can run `bd ready --json` safely.
3. Each child issue includes concrete description, design, acceptance criteria, metadata, and labels for scheduling.
description:
Create a dedicated workstream for repository-level automation using Lefthook, Biome/Ultracite, and Rust tooling so coding agents follow a consistent SDLC path from commit to review to approval.

Current state: `lefthook.yml` is effectively empty, Rust toolchain lint/format standards are not pinned, and no enforced hook messaging exists for post-commit review behavior. This causes drift in quality checks, inconsistent agent behavior, and unclear handoff expectations.

This epic defines the tactical chain needed to activate hooks, enforce quality gates, and guide agents through commit-for-review behavior without overloading `AGENTS.md` with procedural detail.
design:
Treat this as a tooling-contract epic with `lefthook.yml` as the central integration surface.

Implementation should proceed in dependency order: establish Rust standards and core lefthook config first, then install and document behavior, then layer advanced tasks (clippy tuning, fix task, Vitest hook, CI parity, post-commit review reminder).

Keep TypeScript standards aligned with Ultracite/Biome and explicitly document that TS tests run via Vitest when those tests exist. Preserve existing Beads hook integration by invoking `bd hooks run <hook>` within Lefthook jobs instead of dropping tracker automation.

Policy addendum (tracker-first): after any child issue that enables or runs TypeScript (Biome/Ultracite/Vitest/type-check) or Rust (fmt/clippy/check/test) quality gates, agents must first document surfaced problems as tracker issues under this epic before starting remediation work.

2. `rustfmt.toml` exists with explicit, documented formatting settings.

close_reason: Added rust-toolchain.toml (stable channel with rustfmt/clippy components, host target auto-detection) and rustfmt.toml (edition 2021, max_width 100, field init/try shunts). Applied formatting to existing Rust code. Fixed test module scope. All acceptance criteria satisfied.
description:
Rust code in `apps/web/src-tauri` currently has no pinned toolchain file and no repo-level formatting contract. This makes `cargo fmt` and `cargo clippy` behavior inconsistent across machines and agents.

We need a deterministic baseline so hook-based Rust checks are reliable in every worktree.

Files to add: `rust-toolchain.toml` and `rustfmt.toml` at repo root (or justified alternative with clear rationale).
design:
Create two files at the repository root (not inside apps/web/src-tauri/) so they apply to all Rust code in the repo.

**rust-toolchain.toml**: Pin the stable channel with rustfmt and clippy components. The existing Cargo.toml already specifies rust-version = "1.77.2" — the toolchain file must be compatible. Use aarch64-apple-darwin as the target since this is a macOS-only app running on Apple Silicon.

Recommended starting point:
  [toolchain]
  channel = "stable"
  components = ["rustfmt", "clippy"]
  targets = ["aarch64-apple-darwin"]

**rustfmt.toml**: Define formatting rules compatible with Rust edition 2021. The Rust community convention is 4-space indentation (not tabs, even though TS in this repo uses tabs via Biome). Keep it simple and conventional.

Recommended starting point:
  edition = "2021"
  max_width = 100
  use_field_init_shorthand = true
  use_try_shorthand = true

Do NOT change any runtime behavior, Cargo.toml dependencies, or source code. This issue is strictly tooling config. After creating both files, verify by running:
  cd apps/web/src-tauri && cargo fmt -- --check
  cd apps/web/src-tauri && cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

If cargo fmt produces formatting diffs, fix them in a follow-up or include the reformatted code in this PR. If clippy produces warnings, note them but do not suppress them — those are addressed by stt-fys.7 (clippy.toml tuning).

This issue produces the Rust tooling contract consumed by stt-fys.2 (lefthook.yml), stt-fys.7 (clippy.toml), and stt-fys.8 (fix task).
notes: Created rust-toolchain.toml pinning stable channel with rustfmt and clippy components, target aarch64-apple-darwin (macOS Apple Silicon). rustfmt.toml uses edition 2021, max_width 100, use_field_init_shorthand, use_try_shorthand (Rust community defaults). Formatting changes applied to existing code (cargo fmt). Clippy warnings exist but are not suppressed; they will be addressed in stt-fys.7 (clippy.toml tuning) and follow-up issues.
title: Add rustfmt.toml and rust-toolchain.toml for Rust standards

acceptance_criteria:
1. After successful commit, terminal output includes a clear review reminder message.
2. Message explicitly states: do not close the issue before approval.
3. Message requests required summary fields: epic, issue ID, branch name, commit hash.
4. Guidance includes next-step commands for post-approval closure and next issue lookup.
description:
Agents should receive immediate terminal guidance after successful commit/push so they consistently request review and wait for approval before closing Beads issues.

This issue adds explicit `post-commit` reminder output that reinforces required review flow and required summary fields (epic, issue ID, branch, commit hash).
design:
Add a post-commit section to lefthook.yml with a single job named review-reminder. This job runs a shell script that extracts context from git state and prints a structured message that agents will see in their terminal output.

The script must:
1. Extract BRANCH via git branch --show-current
2. Extract HASH via git rev-parse --short HEAD
3. Extract SUBJECT via git log -1 --format='%s'
4. Parse ISSUE_ID from the subject using pattern stt-[a-z0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)? (the beads ID format)
5. Print a visually distinct block (use box-drawing chars or ═ lines) containing:
   - Commit metadata: branch, hash, subject, and parsed issue ID
   - NEXT STEPS numbered 1-3:
     1. Push to remote: git push -u origin HEAD
     2. STOP and prompt the user for review — provide a summary of what was built, the epic ID, issue ID, branch name, and commit hash
     3. WAIT for human approval before proceeding — DO NOT close the issue, DO NOT start the next issue
   - AFTER APPROVAL section (only if issue ID was parsed): show the exact bd close and bd list --epic commands

The message format should look like:
  ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    COMMIT SUCCESSFUL
  ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    Branch:  <branch>
    Commit:  <hash>
    Subject: <subject>
    Issue:   <issue-id>

    NEXT STEPS:
    1. Push to remote: git push -u origin HEAD
    2. STOP and prompt the user for review. Provide:
       - A summary of what you built and why
       - The epic ID, issue ID, branch name, and commit hash
       - What to look at during review
    3. WAIT for human approval before proceeding.
       DO NOT close the issue. DO NOT start the next issue.

    AFTER APPROVAL (not before):
       bd close <issue-id> --reason="<what you built>"
       bd list --epic <EPIC_ID> --status open
  ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

This is a non-blocking hook (post-commit exit code is ignored by git), so it purely provides guidance. Its value is that the agent's LLM reads terminal output and follows these instructions.

IMPORTANT: This job should be added to lefthook.yml as part of a post-commit hook section. If stt-fys.2 is being worked simultaneously, coordinate to avoid merge conflicts on lefthook.yml.
title: Add post-commit review-reminder hook messaging for agents

acceptance_criteria:
1. `cmd_start` in `~/.config/opencode/task.sh` installs Lefthook hooks automatically when `lefthook.yml` exists in the repo root.
2. The install step warns but does not abort worktree creation if it fails.
3. The step is silently skipped (with a clear log line) when `lefthook.yml` is absent.
4. `cmd_stop` and `cmd_nuke` are unchanged.
5. Verified: a freshly created worktree contains active Lefthook-managed hooks in `.git/hooks/`.
description:
task.sh start creates worktrees and configures beads automatically, but does not install Lefthook hooks. This means every agent entering a new worktree must remember to run `lefthook install` (or `bun run hooks:install`) manually before their first commit — a tribal-knowledge step that creates silent gaps in quality gate enforcement.

This issue eliminates that manual step by adding `lefthook install` to `cmd_start` in `task.sh`, so hooks are active in every worktree at creation time without agent intervention.

The file to edit is `~/.config/opencode/task.sh` — this is a global tool, not a file inside the stt repo.
design:
Edit `~/.config/opencode/task.sh`.

Insert the following block into `cmd_start`, after the beads redirect verification block (the `→ Verifying beads access from worktree` section, which ends at the closing `fi` around line 95) and before the Dolt remote check (the `→ Checking Dolt remote configuration` section):

```bash
  # Install Lefthook hooks in the worktree if lefthook.yml is present
  echo "→ Installing Lefthook hooks"
  if [[ -f "${root}/lefthook.yml" ]]; then
    (cd "$wt_path" && bunx lefthook install) && echo "  ✓ Lefthook hooks installed" || echo "  ! Lefthook install failed — run 'bunx lefthook install' manually from the worktree"
  else
    echo "  - lefthook.yml not found in repo root — skipping hook install"
  fi
```

The guard (`[[ -f "${root}/lefthook.yml" ]]`) makes the step a no-op in repos that don't use Lefthook, keeping task.sh safe as a global tool. The install failure path warns but does not exit 1 — a broken hook install should not block worktree creation.

Do NOT add a corresponding uninstall to `cmd_stop`. Lefthook hooks live in `.git/hooks/` which is cleaned up when the worktree is removed by git itself.

After editing, verify by running `task.sh start` in a test repo and confirming the output includes the Lefthook install step and that `.git/hooks/pre-commit` exists in the created worktree.
title: Add lefthook install to task.sh cmd_start

acceptance_criteria:
1. `lefthook.yml` defines non-empty jobs for all required hook stages.
2. Pre-commit and pre-push gates are executable and fail on quality violations.
3. Commit message validation enforces expected commit format.
4. Beads hook integration remains active through Lefthook jobs.
5. Hook output clearly identifies what failed and what command to run next.
close_reason: Populated lefthook.yml with complete hook lifecycle: pre-commit (biome piped group, cargo checks, env/debug guards, beads), commit-msg (conventional commit + subject length), pre-push (branch protection, ultracite, types, cargo, build), post-checkout/merge (conditional dep install), post-commit stub. Fixed post-checkout arg order and biome sequencing.
description:
`lefthook.yml` is currently a placeholder and does not enforce the desired SDLC lifecycle for coding agents.

We need a complete hook contract that covers: pre-commit quality gates, commit-message policy, pre-push full checks, and lifecycle hooks for checkout/merge hygiene.

The config must align with Biome + Ultracite for TS and cargo-based checks for Rust.
design:
Replace the placeholder lefthook.yml with a complete configuration. Use the Lefthook v2 jobs syntax (not the older commands syntax). Set glob_matcher: doublestar at the top level for standard glob behavior.

HOOK STAGES AND JOBS:

1. pre-commit (parallel: true) — fast checks on staged files only:
   - biome-check: run bunx biome check --no-errors-on-unmatched --colors=off {staged_files} with glob *.{ts,tsx,js,jsx,json,css}
   - biome-format: run bunx biome format --no-errors-on-unmatched --colors=off {staged_files} with same glob
   - cargo-fmt-check: run cargo fmt -- --check with glob **/*.rs and root apps/web/src-tauri/
   - cargo-clippy: run cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings with glob **/*.rs and root apps/web/src-tauri/
   - beads-pre-commit: run bd hooks run pre-commit (guard with command -v bd check)
   - no-env-files: block .env files from commit (glob *.env*, exit 1 with clear error)
   - no-debug-artifacts: check staged files for .only or .skip test markers

2. commit-msg — enforce conventional commit format:
   - conventional-commit: validate first line matches regex ^(feat|fix|chore|refactor|docs|test|style|perf|ci|build|revert)(\(.+\))?: .+ using {1} for the message file path
   - subject-line-length: reject subjects over 100 characters
   - beads-prepare-commit-msg: run bd hooks run prepare-commit-msg (guarded)

3. pre-push (parallel: true) — thorough quality gate (the "landing the plane" check):
   - protect-branches: block direct push to main or develop branches, exit 1 with error
   - ultracite-check: run bun run check
   - type-check: run bun run check-types
   - cargo-check: run cargo check --all-targets --all-features with root apps/web/src-tauri/
   - cargo-test: run cargo test --all-features with root apps/web/src-tauri/
   - build: run bun run build
   - beads-pre-push: run bd hooks run pre-push (guarded)

4. post-checkout — environment maintenance:
   - install-deps: if branch checkout (flag=1) and bun.lock or package.json changed between old/new HEAD, run bun install
   - beads-post-checkout: run bd hooks run post-checkout (guarded)

5. post-merge — environment maintenance after pulls:
   - install-deps: if bun.lock, package.json, Cargo.lock, or Cargo.toml changed between HEAD@{1} and HEAD, run bun install
   - beads-post-merge: run bd hooks run post-merge (guarded)

6. post-commit — review reminder (see stt-fys.11 for the specific script)

BEADS INTEGRATION PATTERN:
All beads jobs must guard with: if command -v bd >/dev/null 2>&1; then BD_GIT_HOOK=1 bd hooks run <hook> "$@"; fi
This preserves tracker sync without hard-failing when bd is not installed.

OUTPUT CONFIGURATION:
Set output to show: meta, summary, success, failure, execution, execution_out, skips

IMPORTANT CONSTRAINTS:
- All commands must be deterministic and non-interactive for autonomous agents
- Use {staged_files} in pre-commit for speed; use full project commands in pre-push for thoroughness
- Keep the Rust root: setting pointing to apps/web/src-tauri/ since Cargo.toml lives there
- The protect-branches job must check git branch --show-current and block main/develop

acceptance_criteria:
1. In `hold` mode, left and right activation chords both perform press-to-start and release-to-stop behavior with a single finalization per utterance.
2. In `toggle` mode, the first activation chord starts capture and the next activation chord stops it without requiring key release.
3. In `toggle` mode, silence auto-stop ends capture after the configured `silence_seconds` interval using the active `silence_rms` threshold.
4. Updating `left_chord` and `right_chord` preferences while the app is running immediately changes which chords can start capture.
5. Updating silence preferences while the app is running affects subsequent toggle captures without an app restart.
6. Manual stress testing does not show duplicate typing, duplicate finalization, or a stale watcher stopping a newly started toggle session.
7. QA notes record the tested environment, exact preference values used, observed results, and any follow-up bugs needed.
description:
Run a focused manual QA pass for the push-to-talk runtime on a real macOS machine after the `stt-1j5` toggle-mode implementation and race-condition follow-up landed on `ptt-runtime`.

This QA task is intentionally narrower than the existing broad manual QA issue. It should validate the real runtime path in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs` and `apps/web/src-tauri/src/audio.rs` for hotkey handling, session transitions, silence-based auto-stop, and runtime preference updates.

Scope of this QA pass:
- Hold mode start/stop behavior using the configured activation chord(s)
- Toggle mode start/stop behavior using repeated activation chords
- Silence auto-stop behavior using `silence_seconds` and `silence_rms`
- Runtime application of `left_chord` / `right_chord` preference changes without restarting the app
- Regression coverage for the previously reviewed finalize races: duplicate stop triggers should not cause duplicate typing, duplicate entries, or a stale watcher stopping a newer session

Environment and setup expectations:
- Use a real macOS machine with microphone input available
- Accessibility permission granted for typing output
- Microphone permission granted
- Run the app from the `ptt-runtime` branch at or after commit `fb17edf`
- Prefer a simple target app for observation such as TextEdit, Notes, or a plain textarea in the STT UI
- Use speech samples long enough to generate observable typed output and short enough to repeat reliably during QA

Test script:
1. Launch the app and verify the expected model is loaded and the app is otherwise idle before beginning runtime checks.
2. In preferences, set mode to `hold`, enable both `left_chord` and `right_chord`, and note the current `silence_seconds` / `silence_rms` values.
3. In a target text field, hold the left activation chord, speak a short phrase, release, and verify capture stops only on release, transcription happens once, and text is typed once with no duplicated output.
4. Repeat the same hold-mode flow with the right activation chord and confirm behavior matches the left chord.
5. While still in hold mode, start capture and verify that merely pausing speech does not stop capture before hotkey release; release should remain the stop trigger.
6. Switch to `toggle` mode. In a target text field, press the activation chord once to start capture, speak a short phrase, then press the same chord again to stop. Verify the session stops on the second activation, types once, and does not require key release to end.
7. In `toggle` mode, start capture again, do not press the stop chord, become silent, and verify capture stops automatically only after the configured `silence_seconds` threshold has elapsed.
8. Change `silence_rms` between low, medium, and high using a repeatable environment if possible. Verify the silence-stop sensitivity changes in the expected direction and that the runtime applies the new threshold without requiring an app restart.
9. Change `silence_seconds` to a noticeably shorter value, start a new toggle capture, and verify auto-stop occurs sooner. Then change it back to a longer value and verify the next capture respects the updated timeout.
10. While the app is running, disable `left_chord` and verify the left chord no longer starts capture while the right chord still does.
11. Re-enable `left_chord`, disable `right_chord`, and verify the inverse behavior.
12. Re-enable both chords and verify both work again without restarting the app.
13. Regression check: in `toggle` mode, attempt to press the stop chord around the same moment silence timeout would fire. Repeat several times if needed. Verify each utterance is finalized exactly once: one transcription, one typed output, one logical session stop.
14. Regression check: start a toggle session, stop it, then quickly start a new toggle session. Verify the new session is not prematurely stopped by leftover silence-watch state from the previous session.
15. If logs, session history, or entry history are available in the app, inspect them after the above steps to confirm there are no obvious duplicate entries for a single utterance and that hold vs toggle sessions appear with the expected mode.

Evidence to capture in the issue comments or close reason:
- macOS version used
- Target app(s) used for typing verification
- Which model profile and key preference values were tested
- Whether left and right chords were both verified live
- Whether silence timeout was observed at more than one threshold/timeout setting
- Whether the duplicate-finalize and stale-watcher regressions were stress-tested
- Any failures, flakiness, or timing-sensitive behavior observed

If QA fails, record:
- exact reproduction steps
- whether the failure happened in hold or toggle mode
- whether the failure was tied to left chord, right chord, or both
- whether duplicate typing, duplicate entries, missed stop, premature stop, or stale watcher behavior was observed
- any relevant logs or screenshots
design:
This is a human-run QA task under the `manual-qa` workstream, focused specifically on the PTT runtime lane rather than the full product. It should validate the end-to-end behavior that static review cannot fully prove: real hotkey handling, actual microphone capture timing, silence timeout behavior in a live environment, and runtime preference propagation while the app is already running.

Target implementation areas:
- `apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`
- `apps/web/src-tauri/src/audio.rs`
- hotkey settings exposed through preferences/UI and applied by `KeysHandle::set_enabled()`

Use the `stt-1j5` implementation as the primary dependency and validate the race-condition fixes introduced in follow-up commit `fb17edf`. This task should not block on unrelated full-product QA areas like record-mode route wiring or voice-command coverage unless those areas are needed incidentally to perform the runtime checks above.

Prefer running the checklist in a quiet environment first, then repeat selected toggle/silence checks in a slightly noisier environment if practical so the silence threshold behavior is exercised under less ideal conditions.
title: Manual QA: verify hold/toggle hotkey runtime on macOS

acceptance_criteria:
1. Accessibility checks do not send destructive keystrokes to the frontmost app.
2. Microphone checks use a safer and more reliable mechanism than the current AppleScript shell hacks, or clearly degrade to `Undetermined` without side effects.
3. Request/open-settings flows remain available when direct checks are not possible.
4. Manual permission QA no longer risks mutating user content.
close_reason: Replaced destructive permission probes with safe macOS checks: accessibility now uses AXIsProcessTrusted() API, microphone uses explicit Undetermined fallback with settings-based onboarding
description: Permission handling in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/permissions.rs` currently relies on brittle AppleScript shell hacks and a destructive accessibility probe. `check_accessibility()` sends `Cmd+X` to the frontmost app, which can mutate or remove user content, and microphone checks rely on brittle `osascript`/shell behavior that may produce false positives or false negatives.
design: Rework permissions handling around safe, side-effect-free checks and guided onboarding. Avoid synthesizing destructive keystrokes, centralize permission-state reads, and keep the onboarding/open-settings flow explicit when direct status checks are not trustworthy. Treat any fallback shelling as best-effort only after the safe path is exhausted.
title: Replace destructive permission probes with safe macOS checks

acceptance_criteria:
1. RecordCapture in audio.rs correctly captures continuous audio for multi-hour sessions (stt-bq4 closed).
2. File rotation triggers correctly at maxHours (8h) and maxFileGB (4GB) bounds with no data loss; buffer_start_time reset bug and update_file_size stub are resolved (stt-rdp closed).
3. The /record route invokes real Tauri commands (start_record_mode, stop_record_mode, transcribe_record_chunk, get_record_status) instead of raw DB calls; UI state reflects actual capture state (stt-fuc closed).
4. Unit tests lock in chunking and rotation behavior (stt-83i closed).
5. Unused hound crate is removed from Cargo.toml (stt-vxq.1 closed).
6. Transcript files are written correctly to ~/Library/Application Support/com.stt.sst/transcripts/{session_id}.txt and session rows in the DB reflect real record-mode captures.
description: Owns the long-form recording path end-to-end: backend RecordCapture in audio.rs for continuous multi-hour capture, chunked transcription every 60 seconds via whisper-rs, safe file rotation when maxHours (8h) or maxFileGB (4GB) bounds are reached, /record route wiring to the real start_record_mode/stop_record_mode/transcribe_record_chunk/get_record_status commands, and unit coverage for chunking and rotation. Currently the /record route creates sessions via raw DB calls instead of using the dedicated record-mode Tauri commands, so the UI can claim recording has started while no actual audio capture or transcription is happening. RecordCapture exists in audio.rs but rotation logic (check_rotation_needed, update_file_size) has stubs and edge cases. Transcript files are written to ~/Library/Application Support/com.stt.sst/transcripts/{session_id}.txt.
design: Record mode is architecturally separate from hold/toggle PTT: it uses RecordCapture (not AudioCapture/AudioHandle), writes to files and DB (never types into apps), and has its own Tauri commands. The chain is: stt-bq4 (capture correctness) -> stt-rdp (rotation correctness including buffer_start_time reset bug and update_file_size stub) -> stt-fuc (wire /record route to real commands instead of raw DB calls) -> stt-83i (unit tests lock in behavior). The hound crate in Cargo.toml is unused and should be removed (stt-vxq.1).
title: Workstream: record-mode
